name: Manufacturing & Production

users.context: Executes work orders at a machine or station; records production quantity, scrap, and downtime; scans material and job travelers. Works standing, often gloved, in noisy environments with industrial glare on screens.
users.expertise: Domain-expert at the physical process, low-to-moderate computer fluency. Needs large touch targets, minimal typing, and forgiving input.

users.context: Builds the master production schedule, runs MRP, balances machine capacity against demand, releases and sequences work orders. Juggles material shortages and rush orders.
users.expertise: Expert in MRP/capacity planning and BOM logic; high tool fluency with Gantt and sequencing views.

users.context: Performs incoming, in-process, and final inspection against specs; records measurements, raises non-conformance reports (NCRs), enforces hold/quarantine. Moves between lab bench and line.
users.expertise: Expert in inspection plans, sampling, SPC, and quality standards (ISO 9001, IATF 16949).

users.context: Executes preventive and breakdown maintenance work orders, logs parts consumed and labour, updates asset condition. Mobile around the plant, hands often dirty.
users.expertise: Expert in equipment and CMMS workflows; intermediate computer skills, prefers mobile/tablet at the asset.

users.context: Manages raw material, WIP, and finished goods; performs goods receipt, picking, stock transfers, and cycle counts. Barcode/RF scanner is the primary input device.
users.expertise: Expert in stock movements, lot/serial traceability, and bin management.

users.context: Monitors OEE, throughput, and on-time delivery; reviews exceptions and approves overtime or expedites. Wall-mounted andon dashboards plus mobile glance.
users.expertise: Expert in operations; consumes summarised real-time KPIs, drills into bottlenecks.

workflows.name: Work order execution and production reporting
steps: Receive and accept released work order
Stage and confirm material/components (backflush or manual issue)
Run operation and record good quantity, scrap, and reason codes
Log machine downtime and changeover
Complete operation and move WIP to next station
Close work order and post finished goods to stock

workflows.name: MRP run and work-order release
steps: Confirm demand (sales orders, forecasts)
Run material requirements planning
Review planned orders and exception messages
Check material and capacity availability
Convert planned orders to work/purchase orders
Sequence and release to the floor

workflows.name: Quality inspection and non-conformance handling
steps: Trigger inspection at receipt/in-process/final
Record measurements against inspection plan
Accept, reject, or place on hold
Raise NCR and quarantine affected lot
Disposition (rework, scrap, use-as-is, return to vendor)
Close NCR with corrective action reference

workflows.name: Preventive maintenance work order
steps: Generate PM schedule from meter/time triggers
Assign technician and reserve spare parts
Lockout/tagout and perform task checklist
Record parts consumed and labour hours
Update asset condition and next-due
Close and return equipment to service

workflows.name: Goods receipt and lot traceability
steps: Scan inbound delivery against PO
Inspect and record lot/serial and quantity
Put away to bin location
Maintain genealogy link from raw lot to finished batch
Enable forward/backward trace for recall

risks: Wrong quantity or scrap reason booked, distorting inventory and yield metrics
Building against an outdated Bill of Materials or routing revision
Releasing a work order without material/capacity, starving the line or causing rework
Shipping product that should have been on quality hold (escaped defect)
Broken lot/serial genealogy preventing an effective recall
Maintenance performed without lockout-tagout, creating a safety hazard
Negative or phantom inventory from mis-sequenced backflush
Mis-scan of part or lot leading to wrong-material consumption
Stale real-time dashboard causing a wrong scheduling decision
Operator mis-tap on a glare-washed touchscreen recording the wrong machine state
environment_constraints: Gloved hands and protective equipment reduce touch precision
High ambient noise rules out audio-only feedback
Screen glare and dust on industrial/ruggedised terminals
Operators standing, mobile, and time-pressured per cycle time
Barcode/RF scanners and kiosks rather than mouse-and-keyboard
Intermittent network in metal-heavy plant areas requiring offline tolerance
Safety-critical states (lockout, e-stop) must be unmistakable
domain_patterns: Master-detail (work order header with operation/component lines)
Kiosk single-task mode with large targets
audit_compliance: FDA 21 CFR Part 820 for medical-device manufacturing
accessibility_concerns: Large touch targets and gesture tolerance for gloved operation
High-contrast, anti-glare UI legible under harsh plant lighting
Status must not depend on colour alone (running/down/hold also need icon/text)
Haptic or large visual feedback since audio is unreliable in noise
Minimise required typing; favour scan, tap, and reason-code pick lists
Readable at distance for wall-mounted andon displays
anti_patterns: Tiny buttons and dense forms on a shop-floor kiosk
Colour-only machine-state encoding washed out by glare
Requiring keyboard text entry from gloved operators
Allowing production against an unreleased or superseded BOM revision
Silent inventory go-negative instead of blocking/flagging
Audio-only alerts in a high-noise environment
Burying the hold/quarantine action so defective lots flow downstream
No offline buffer, so a network blip loses a shift's production counts
